report_name: Malicious TronLink Chrome Extension Uses Double-Layer Phishing to Steal Cryptocurrency Wallets

attack_patterns.name: Phishing for Information
attack_patterns.description: The malicious extension employed a two-layer phishing attack, presenting a fake interface mimicking the real TronLink wallet to trick users into entering their sensitive mnemonic phrases, private keys, and passwords.

attack_patterns.name: Software Extensions
attack_patterns.description: The attack was delivered via a malicious Google Chrome extension that impersonated the official TronLink wallet to persist on the user's browser and conduct its credential harvesting attack.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Stolen wallet credentials were exfiltrated over the command and control channel established with an attacker-controlled Telegram bot.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The malicious extension used Unicode characters in its name to obfuscate its identity and appear identical to the legitimate 'TronLink' extension, thereby evading user detection.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Stolen credentials were exfiltrated from the compromised browser using web protocols to an attacker-controlled Telegram bot API.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The malicious extension was distributed by compromising the software supply chain, likely by being published to the official Chrome Web Store and evading automated checks, or through third-party sites.

malware.name: Fake TronLink Chrome Extension
malware.description: A malicious Google Chrome extension (ID: ekjidonhjmneoompmjbjofpjmhklpjdd) that impersonates the legitimate TronLink wallet. It uses a two-layer phishing interface to steal cryptocurrency wallet credentials like mnemonic phrases and private keys, then exfiltrates them to a Telegram bot.

tools.name: Telegram
tools.description: Used as the command and control (C2) and data exfiltration channel. Stolen wallet credentials were sent to an attacker-controlled Telegram bot.

campaigns.name: Fake TronLink Extension Phishing Campaign
campaigns.description: A phishing campaign using a malicious Google Chrome extension to impersonate the TronLink wallet and steal cryptocurrency credentials from TRON users.
campaigns.objective: Steal cryptocurrency wallet credentials for financial gain.

identities.name: TronLink
identities.description: The legitimate TRON cryptocurrency wallet application that was impersonated by the malicious Chrome extension.

identities.name: TRON cryptocurrency users
identities.description: The targeted victim group for this phishing campaign, who were tricked into providing their wallet credentials.

infrastructure.name: Fake TronLink C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Domains used by the malicious extension to host phishing components and for command and control.

infrastructure.name: Telegram C2 Bot
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled Telegram bot used as the destination for exfiltrated wallet credentials.

indicators.name: Fake TronLink Extension C2 Domains
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Fake TronLink Extension C2 DNS Query
id: 2b8c1f9a-e5d7-4f8b-9c1a-3e4d5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with a malicious TronLink Chrome extension used for phishing crypto credentials.
references:
  - https://www.techflowpost.com/slowmist_discloses_phishing_campaign_involving_fake_tronlink_chrome_extension_that_steals_wallet_credentials_such_as_mnemonics_and_private_keys_18635.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'tronfind-api.tronfindexplorer.com'
      - 'trx-scan-explorer.org'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to command and control domains used by a malicious fake TronLink Chrome extension.

indicators.name: Fake TronLink Malicious Chrome Extension ID
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Fake TronLink Malicious Chrome Extension Installation
id: 8d9e0f1a-b2c3-4d5e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of a malicious fake TronLink Chrome extension by its unique ID in the user's file system.
references:
  - https://www.techflowpost.com/slowmist_discloses_phishing_campaign_involving_fake_tronlink_chrome_extension_that_steals_wallet_credentials_such_as_mnemonics_and_private_keys_18635.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1176
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains|all:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data'
      - '\Extensions\ekjidonhjmneoompmjbjofpjmhklpjdd'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the installation of the malicious Chrome extension by its specific ID `ekjidonhjmneoompmjbjofpjmhklpjdd`.

indicators.name: Potential C2 Traffic to Telegram API
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Potential C2 Traffic to Telegram API from Browser
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-4a1b-8c2d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects network connections to the Telegram API domain from browser processes, which could indicate data exfiltration by malicious extensions. This may generate false positives in environments where Telegram web clients are used.'
references:
  - https://www.techflowpost.com/slowmist_discloses_phishing_campaign_involving_fake_tronlink_chrome_extension_that_steals_wallet_credentials_such_as_mnemonics_and_private_keys_18635.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname: 'api.telegram.org'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network connections to api.telegram.org, which was used for data exfiltration. This may have false positives.
